task: Launch-quality advanced task: Transformer Dynamic Loading Thermal Margin. Reality context: a utility considers temporary overload during feeder reconfiguration. Embedded packet: metric map: metric1=load_pct, metric2=ambient_C, metric3=top_oil_C, metric4=loss_of_life_hours; records: R1 load_pct=15.8 ambient_C=65.9 top_oil_C=70 loss_of_life_hours=350906; R2 load_pct=54.6 ambient_C=16 top_oil_C=70.2 loss_of_life_hours=164341; R3 load_pct=46.6 ambient_C=39.6 top_oil_C=76 loss_of_life_hours=131843; R4 load_pct=76 ambient_C=46.7 top_oil_C=53.2 loss_of_life_hours=515550; limit_load_pct=36; limit_ambient_C=48.9; limit_top_oil_C=75.5; decision_budget_usd=160000; sensitivity_shift_pct=5; hard_threshold_record_count=1; removed_context="benchmark framing favored long-horizon professional work with concrete verification". Rules and constraints: Treat confidence below 0.70, missing provenance, or contradictory record status as needs_review even when the numeric score passes. Options or recommendations cannot use external measurements, specialized licensed software, or hidden assumptions; all calculations must use only the embedded packet, and any contradictory or under-supported record must be marked needs_review. Required work: compute thermal margin and loading policy; convert the packet into an auditable evidence table, cite the governing values, and list unresolved assumptions. Deliver transformer_loading_margin.xlsx + dispatch_note.md with formulas or executable logic, record-level citations, validation checks, sensitivity notes, and a final recommendation table suitable for expert review.
